shared_model_schema:
QUERY GROUNDING (Power BI, an existing import query to clean).

CURRENT QUERY 'Sales' (M):
let
    Source = Excel.Workbook(File.Contents("C:\\data\\sales.xlsx"), null, true),
    Sheet = Source{[Item="Sheet1", Kind="Sheet"]}[Data],
    #"Promoted Headers" = Table.PromoteHeaders(Sheet, [PromoteAllScalars=true])
in
    #"Promoted Headers"

OUTPUT COLUMNS of 'Sales' (all currently typed text/any):
  [Order ID] (text)
  [Customer Number] (text)
  [Order Date] (text)        -- looks like M/D/YYYY strings
  [Region] (text)            -- has leading/trailing spaces and some blanks
  [Quantity] (text)          -- numeric-looking text
  [Amount] (text)            -- numeric-looking text

OTHER REFERENCEABLE QUERIES / PARAMETERS:
  'Dim_Region'  (table query)

evals.name: clean-blanks-types
evals.prompt:
Using the query grounding below, clean the 'Sales' query: drop rows where Customer Number is blank, trim the Region text, and set proper data types (Order Date as a date, Quantity as a whole number, Amount as a decimal number). Build on the existing query and explain briefly.

<QUERY_GROUNDING>
evals.expected_output: A full let ... in query that starts from #"Promoted Headers" (does NOT rewrite the Excel.Workbook source), then: filters with each [Customer Number] <> null and [Customer Number] <> ""; trims Region via Table.TransformColumns(..., {{"Region", Text.Trim, type text}}); and sets types LAST via Table.TransformColumnTypes with a culture (e.g. "en-US") mapping Order Date->type date, Quantity->Int64.Type, Amount->type number. Power BI-style step names. References only existing columns.

evals.name: dedupe-and-merge
evals.prompt:
On the 'Sales' query below, remove fully duplicate rows, then bring in the Region's description from the Dim_Region query by joining on Region. Build on the existing query.

<QUERY_GROUNDING>
evals.expected_output: A query that appends onto the existing last step: Table.Distinct for full-row dedupe; then Table.NestedJoin(prev, {"Region"}, #"Dim_Region", {"Region"}, "dim", JoinKind.LeftOuter) followed by Table.ExpandTableColumn to surface the description column. References #"Dim_Region" (a real query in the grounding) and only existing columns. Does not rewrite the source.

evals.name: robust-numeric-conversion
evals.prompt:
The Amount column in 'Sales' is text and sometimes contains junk like 'N/A' or empty strings. Convert it to a number safely without breaking the refresh, and drop rows where it can't be converted. Use the grounding below.

<QUERY_GROUNDING>
evals.expected_output: Handles errors deliberately rather than a bare type ascription: e.g. Table.TransformColumns(prev, {{"Amount", each try Number.From(_) otherwise null, type number}}) (or replace 'N/A'/"" with null first), then Table.SelectRows / Table.RemoveRowsWithErrors to drop the unconvertible rows. Explains that a plain TransformColumnTypes would leave Error cells that propagate into the model. Builds on the existing query, real columns only.
